key_insight: DG-FDD improves domain-incremental remote sensing change detection by explicitly adapting to bitemporal feature differences and distilling knowledge in the frequency domain to reduce forgetting.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical continual-learning strategy for deploying change-detection models across new sensing domains while preserving prior performance without storing historical data.
method: Difference-Guided Dynamic Adapter (DGDA) learns change-aware feature adaptation from bitemporal discrepancies.
Frequency-Decoupled Knowledge Distillation with Cross-domain Synthesis (FDKD-CS) separates structural content from domain style in the frequency domain.
Designed for domain-incremental RSCD to balance plasticity for new domains and stability for old domains.
evidence_signal: Across six two-domain sequences and three three-domain sequences, DG-FDD kept mean relative changes near zero, with F1/IoU drops of only -0.23%/-0.45% and -0.69%/-1.31%, respectively, versus single-task models.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of frequency-domain separation and adapter tuning, so robustness across sensors, resolutions, and strongly shifted domains still needs careful validation.
paper_title: Domain-Incremental Remote Sensing Change Detection via Difference-Guided Adaptation and Frequency-Decoupled Distillation
